machinery_genes.role: first gene of the virB type-IV secretion operon; operon promoter / lytic transglycosylase family
machinery_genes.quote: the B. abortus virB locus constitutes an operon transcribed from virB1 which is turned on during the stationary phase of growth
machinery_genes.note: Exact-genome source (B. abortus 2308). virB1 named as the first/promoter-proximal gene of the operon.

machinery_genes.quote: Between virB1 and virB2 and downstream of ORF12, two degenerated, palindromic repeat sequences characteristic of Brucella intergenic regions were found
machinery_genes.note: virB2 named directly; the same sentence names ORF12 as the operon's downstream terminus, bracketing the contiguous virB1-virB12 operon.

machinery_genes.role: IM-OM channel ring; required for correct function of the secretion apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: Mutants with polar and nonpolar mutations introduced in virB10 showed different behaviors in mice and in the HeLa cell infection assay, suggesting that virB10 per se is necessary for the correct function of this type IV secretion apparatus
machinery_genes.note: virB10 (mid-operon) individually characterised; confirms the operon is the functional T4SS apparatus.

notes: HAND-CURATED in the main session: the per-instance subagent was blocked by a spurious Usage-Policy false-positive (Brucella is a CDC select agent), as were T4SS_03, T4SS_06 and T3SS_21. The Brucella VirB apparatus is a single collinear operon: Sieira et al 2000 (exact genome, B. abortus 2308) describe 'a collinear arrangement of 13 open reading frames (ORFs)' transcribed as one operon from virB1, canonically virB1-virB12 (virB1-virB11 homologues of the Agrobacterium pTi VirB proteins plus a 12th lipoprotein ORF; O'Callaghan et al 1999, B. suis: '12 open reading frames (ORFs) encoding homologues of the 11 VirB proteins present in the pTi plasmid of Agrobacterium ... and a twelfth ORF encoding a putative lipoprotein'). Only virB1, virB2 and virB10 are individually quote-anchored here (PARTIAL); they bracket and span the contiguous operon, so the locus is well located even though virB3-virB9, virB11 and the virB12 lipoprotein are not separately quoted. IMPORTANT replicon fact for Phase 1: the B. abortus 2308 virB operon is on chromosome II (BAB2 / accession NC_007624.1), whereas this instance's effectors (BAB1_*) are on chromosome I (NC_007618.1). Effectors and machinery are therefore on different replicons -> out of reach of any same-replicon proximity rule by construction. The resolve step (3.5) must search the organism's chromosome II for these gene names. Translocated effectors (e.g. VceA/VceC, RicA, BPE123) deliberately excluded as cargo, not apparatus.
